subject: Physics
title: Cosmic Microwave Background
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cosmic Microwave Background.

hook.text: In 1964, two radio engineers heard a mysterious hiss from every direction. It was the echo of the Big Bang.

buildup.text: Arno Penzias and Robert Wilson couldn't eliminate the noise. They even cleaned pigeon droppings from their antenna.

discovery.text: The signal was cosmic microwave background radiation—ancient light from when the universe first became transparent.

twist.text: This radiation has cooled to just 2.7 Kelvin over 13.8 billion years. It fills every cubic centimeter of space.

climax.text: Tiny temperature variations in the CMB map out where galaxies would eventually form. It's a baby photo of the cosmos.

punchline.text: An annoying hiss turned out to be the oldest light in existence.

quiz.question: What is the cosmic microwave background?
options: Ancient radiation from when the universe first became transparent
Heat from the Sun reflected by dust
